perturbation.original_text: "Customer" means a third party who purchases the Products from Reseller within the Territory, for its internal use only (including for the personal use of its End Users), such as nursing homes. "End User" means a third party who is a customer of Customer or is otherwise related to a customer of Customer, and who purchases and/or uses the Products within the Territory, for its personal use only.
perturbation.changed_text: "Customer" means any entity that acquires the Products from the Reseller within the Territory. "End User" means a third party who is the direct recipient of the product's benefits, regardless of purchase origin within the Territory, for any use.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Customer' is restricted to purchases for internal use only, while 'End User' is tied to a customer of a Customer. The modified version broadens 'Customer' to *any* entity acquiring the product, eliminating the 'internal use' limitation, and redefines 'End User' to anyone directly benefiting from the product, irrespective of the purchase pathway or usage (removing the personal use only restriction). This creates a contradiction when sections of the contract discuss responsibilities regarding 'Customers' and 'End Users,' as the obligations now apply to a broader set of entities with potentially conflicting interests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Reseller By fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, the Reseller shall: (i) comply with all applicable laws and regulations; (ii) use materials and branding solely as determined by the Supplier (subject to the right of the Reseller to make changes to such materials as are described in Section 2.4); (iii) attend training and sales meetings as required by the Supplier, so as to be able to describe, demonstrate, and sell the Products adequately; (iv) make sure that all the End Users are signing the End User Agreement, and in the event that any End User does not comply with these terms, Reseller shall bear all liability associated with said End User; and (v) make no representations or warranties with respect to the Products other than those provided in the Products' documentation; Supplier shall be fully responsible for shipping the Products to the Reseller at the address of the Reseller specified in each Order (or as otherwise communicated by Reseller to Supplier under this Agreement) and will obtain and provide for itself and/or on behalf of Reseller, any and all registrations, licenses, permits, certificates and all other documentation and information required for the exportation, importation, storage, marketing and otherwise resale of the Products in the Territory. For such process, Supplier will allocate the additional cost on a pre-agreed per Product unit additional price basis, which will be subject to an increase (subject to changes in taxes). Reseller agrees to reimburse Supplier for the reasonable out of pocket costs and expenses incurred by Supplier in connection with the shipment and delivery of the Products, and of obtaining the foregoing registrations, licenses, permits, certificated and other documentation, provided that Supplier submits an invoice therefore in reasonable detail (and unless and solely to the extent such reimbursements is prohibited by Section 14.9 or prohibited under other applicable laws). For the avoidance of doubt, in no way will Supplier be subject to any tax (i.e., sales tax) relating to the sale of the Products to Reseller's Customers and/or End Users.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited 'Customer' to entities using the products for internal use and tied 'End User' to customers of Customers. The changed text broadens 'Customer' to any entity acquiring the product and redefines 'End User' to anyone benefiting from the product, regardless of purchase or use. This directly contradicts the contradicted section, particularly obligation (iv), which requires the Reseller to ensure 'all the End Users are signing the End User Agreement' and bear liability for non-compliance. The new definition of 'End User' is far broader, encompassing potentially unintended parties, which the reseller would now be liable for.

perturbation.original_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories listed in Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories listed in Schedule 1, or as otherwise mutually agreed upon in writing by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Territory' is limited to Schedule 1. The modified definition allows for expansion of the Territory via written agreement. This introduces ambiguity as Sections of the contract that depend on the defined 'Territory' are now subject to an undefined expansion process. It introduces uncertainty about which geographical areas the agreement applies to, particularly concerning sales and marketing rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment During the Term (defined below) and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement (including, but not limited to Section 12.4 hereof), Supplier hereby (i) appoints Reseller, and Reseller accepts the appointment, as a non‐ exclusive reseller of the Products in the Territory. In connection with the foregoing appointment, Supplier hereby grants Reseller a non-transferable, revocable, limited right to resell, market, promote, stimulate interest in, and solicit Orders by Customers and/or End Users in the Territory for the Products and to provide services in connection with those activities. Reseller shall be solely responsible for all of its costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Supplier reserves any and all rights with respect to the Products not expressly granted in this Agreement. Without limiting the right of the Reseller to receive any and all Paid-For Products (including but not limited to under Section 4.2 hereof), nothing in this Agreement shall entitle the Reseller to any priority of supply in relation to the Products as against any other distributor or customer of the Supplier. "Paid‐ For Products" means Products for which Reseller submitted an Order, had that Order accepted by Supplier, and for which Reseller has remitted payment in full to the Supplier under this Agreement. Subject to the terms of Section 13, below, it is hereby clarified that Supplier may, in its sole discretion, sell the Products to any other person or entity, whether within the Territory or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Territory' as explicitly listed in Schedule 1. The changed text expands this definition to include areas 'as otherwise mutually agreed upon in writing by both Parties.' This directly contradicts the 'Appointment During the Term' section, which grants the Reseller rights 'in the Territory.' The ambiguity introduced by the changed definition, allowing for an undefined expansion of the Territory, creates uncertainty about the scope of the Reseller's rights, leading to a clear and strong in-text contradiction. This uncertainty would significantly impact the enforceability and understanding of the reseller agreement, as the areas in which the reseller is authorized to act are now subject to change outside the explicit terms of the original Territory definition.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall place its Orders by email, at the email address set out in Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller shall place its Orders by electronic communication, using methods and addresses as specified in company policies, which may change.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that order placement should be done via email at an address listed in Schedule 1. The modified text now dictates that orders should be placed via electronic communication, as specified in *company policies*, and allows changes to these methods. This change introduces ambiguity because the method and addresses are no longer fixed and are subject to change, which leads to uncertainty about how orders should be placed and whether orders are being processed correctly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Reseller shall place its Orders by electronic communication, using methods and addresses as specified in company policies, which may change. Each Order shall detail the total order quantity of the Product and the requested date of delivery (taking into consideration up to 4 months lead time). Within 5 business days following the receipt of each Order, Supplier shall acknowledge receipt of such Order. No Order shall be binding upon Supplier until such written acknowledgment was sent by Supplier to Reseller; provided that Supplier shall confirm or reject such Orders at its discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that order placement methods and addresses are now specified in 'company policies, which may change.' This directly contradicts the original implication that the order placement process is stable and defined in Schedule 1. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, however, the fact that the order placement policies 'may change' introduces a clear and direct conflict creating uncertainty about how orders should be placed. It creates a strong ambiguity, aligning with the Misaligned Terminology definition that leads to in-text contradictions.
